task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI is launched and configured for a Synthetic board session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible, and the system control panel indicates that a 'Synthetic' board or data source is selected.

task_completion.description: The data stream is started in the OpenBCI GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start Data Stream' button has been clicked, and time-series data (e.g., EEG waveforms) is actively flowing across the graphs.
The 'Stop Data Stream' button has been clicked, and the time-series data flow is paused/frozen on the screen.

task_completion.description: The data stream is restarted after being stopped.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start Data Stream' button is clicked again, and new time-series data resumes flowing across the graphs.

task_completion.description: The required log file is created or opened in a text editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) or terminal shows the file `~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/stream_lifecycle_log.txt` being edited.

task_completion.description: The log file contains all required fields and the correct status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file explicitly contains the lines 'stream_started: <number>', 'stream_stopped: <number>', 'stream_restarted: <number>', and 'status: complete'.

integrity.description: The agent actually interacted with the OpenBCI GUI to perform the stream lifecycle test.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the OpenBCI GUI interface with the stream actively running, stopping, and restarting, rather than the agent just writing arbitrary timestamps into a text file without opening the application.

integrity.description: The agent recorded actual Unix timestamps instead of placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values next to the stream events in the log file are actual numbers (e.g., 10-digit integers like 17... or floats) representing Unix time, not the literal string '<timestamp>'.

integrity.description: The agent waited at least 3 seconds between stopping and restarting the stream.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical difference between the 'stream_restarted' timestamp and the 'stream_stopped' timestamp in the log file is 3 or greater.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Log file must be exactly at `/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/stream_lifecycle_log.txt`. Required fields in the log file: `stream_started`, `stream_stopped`, `stream_restarted`, and `status`. The status value must be exactly `complete`. The timestamps must be Unix timestamps, and the difference between `stream_restarted` and `stream_stopped` must be at least 3 seconds. The OpenBCI GUI must be used in Synthetic (algorithmic) data generation mode.
